message:
test(fuzz): seed merge_issue and sync_cycle corpora with 534 coverage-discovered inputs

Seed the libfuzzer corpus directories for the two new fuzz harnesses
landed earlier on this branch:

  fuzz/corpus/merge_issue/  +283 inputs
  fuzz/corpus/sync_cycle/   +251 inputs

These are real coverage-guided inputs discovered by running the two
harnesses locally; each filename is the sha1 of the input bytes, which
is libfuzzer's standard convention for corpus-dedup. The inputs are
the JSON test vectors the harness deserializes into
(base, left, right) tuples for the merge-issue target and into
(events) for the sync-cycle target.

Why commit them to the repo rather than regenerate from scratch:

1. Reproducibility. A freshly-cloned checkout on CI or another dev
   box starts libfuzzer with an empty corpus and has to re-discover
   every coverage edge. That's minutes-to-hours of wasted CI time
   that can instead start from the pre-discovered corpus and cover
   every already-known edge on the first run.

2. Regression pinning. Any input that previously triggered a
   divergence in the merge-issue 3-way reconciler or a
   non-idempotent step in sync_cycle is captured as a file here —
   reintroducing that class of bug will trip the harness on the very
   first fuzzing iteration instead of after minutes of re-discovery.

3. Convention. The existing `fuzz/corpus/content_hash/` and
   `fuzz/corpus/jsonl_import/` corpora in this repo follow the same
   pattern (committed, sha1-named); keeping the new harnesses'
   corpora visible in git matches the project norm.

Each file is small (typical 100-2000 bytes of JSON) so the
aggregate repo-size impact is modest (~500 KB across both corpora).
None of these files contain secrets or identifying data — they are
synthetic fuzz inputs whose entire purpose is to drive parser / 3-way
merge / sync state-machine branches.

The inputs were filtered through the existing `content_hash` dedup
+ libfuzzer's minimal-corpus merge so the committed set is the
minimal cover rather than the raw queue. Any further inputs added by
a future fuzzing session will be committed incrementally rather than
rebased over this one.
